response:
Welcome back! It's great to chat with you again. Since our previous conversation, we didn't have any information about each other, so let's start fresh!

I'm doing great, thanks for asking! As your friendly AI assistant, I'm happy to engage in helpful conversations while being informative and concise. I can provide information on various topics or help you with a specific question or problem.

If there's something on your mind that you'd like to discuss or explore, feel free to share it! To get us started, would you like to talk about a particular topic, ask me a question, or simply enjoy some conversation? I'm here to listen and assist you in any way I can!

What's been going on in your life lately?
reasoning: The user is initiating a general conversation and expressing positivity.

pipeline.description: Audio is resampled to 16kHz mono, padded/trimmed to 30 seconds, then converted to an 80-channel log-Mel spectrogram using a 25ms window with 10ms stride.
The log-Mel spectrogram passes through 2 Conv1D layers (stride 2 for downsampling), then through 12 transformer encoder layers with self-attention. Sinusoidal positional encodings are added.

The decoder generates text tokens autoregressively using 12 transformer layers. Each layer has: (1) Causal self-attention over generated tokens, (2) Cross-attention to encoder output, (3) Feed-forward network.

details.causal_mask: Prevents attending to future text tokens

pipeline.description: Decoded tokens are detokenized, timestamps are aligned, and the final transcript is cleaned up.

The orchestrator uses Llama 3 (instruction-tuned) to classify the user's intent into one of 5 categories. The model outputs structured JSON thanks to instruction tuning — it has learned to follow specific output formats during fine-tuning.

details.reasoning: The user is initiating a general conversation and expressing positivity.

pipeline.description: Input text is split into tokens (subword units) and converted to integer IDs. Each token maps to a learned embedding vector.
Each token ID is mapped to a dense vector (dimension 4096 for Llama 3 8B). Positional encodings (RoPE) are added to inject sequence order information.

Input passes through 32 transformer decoder layers. Each layer applies: (1) Multi-Head Self-Attention with causal mask, (2) RMSNorm, (3) Feed-Forward Network (SwiGLU activation), (4) Residual connections.
The final hidden states are projected through a linear layer to logits over the vocabulary. Softmax converts logits to probabilities. Tokens are sampled autoregressively until EOS.

Before agent execution, relevant context is retrieved from the vector memory. The user's message is embedded and compared against stored conversation history using cosine similarity in ChromaDB's HNSW index.

details.query: Hey, how are you? I'm good. Just talk to me.

pipeline.description: The search query is embedded into the same 768-dim vector space as stored documents using nomic-embed-text, enabling meaningful distance comparisons.
The HNSW index performs approximate nearest neighbor search using cosine similarity. It traverses the graph from entry point to the query's neighborhood, comparing sim(q, v) = (q·v)/(||q||·||v||).
Based on the classified intent 'general_chat' (confidence: 1.00), the request is routed to the 'General Chat' agent. The agent receives the query plus 5 retrieved context items.
Input text is split into tokens (subword units) and converted to integer IDs. Each token maps to a learned embedding vector.
Each token ID is mapped to a dense vector (dimension 4096 for Llama 3 8B). Positional encodings (RoPE) are added to inject sequence order information.

Input passes through 32 transformer decoder layers. Each layer applies: (1) Multi-Head Self-Attention with causal mask, (2) RMSNorm, (3) Feed-Forward Network (SwiGLU activation), (4) Residual connections.
The final hidden states are projected through a linear layer to logits over the vocabulary. Softmax converts logits to probabilities. Tokens are sampled autoregressively until EOS.
Both the user message and assistant response are embedded and stored in ChromaDB. This builds the persistent conversational memory, enabling future queries to retrieve relevant past interactions.

transcription: Hey, how are you? I'm good. Just talk to me.
